description: Mangalam Worldwide Limited submitted its 30th Annual Report for FY 2025-26, reporting consolidated PAT of ₹5,014.32 Lakhs, up 69.83%, on total income of ₹1,21,498.82 Lakhs, up ~14%. Key milestones include dual-exchange listing on NSE and BSE Main Boards, ₹100 crore NCD issuance, a proposed stock split from ₹10 to ₹1 face value, and a final dividend of ₹0.30 per share. The 30th AGM is scheduled for July 30, 2026.

title: Valuation Watch: 12 mid-cap stocks with sky-high PE ratios
description: A P/E ratio above 100 indicates that a company's stock is trading at more than 100 times its trailing 12-month earnings, often reflecting strong growth expectations or rich valuations. The ratio is calculated using the latest end-of-day stock price and trailing 12-month (TTM) earnings per share. Based on a valuation scan by StockEdge.com, we have highlighted the top 12 stocks from the NSE midcap pack with the highest TTM P/E ratios, using financials up to the March 2026 quarter and excluding banking and financial stocks.While a high P/E suggests strong growth expectations from investors, it can also signal overvaluation or speculative buying. It's important to assess the company's fundamentals and market conditions for a more accurate evaluation. Premium Pricing

description: Pune (Maharashtra) [India], June 29: Bajaj Finance has strengthened its personal loan offering by providing customers with a repayment tenure of up to 108 months. The extended tenure gives eligible borrowers greater flexibility to plan their repayments while meeting a wide range of financial requirements. Customers can apply for a collateral-free loan ranging from Rs. 40,000 to Rs. 55 lakh, with a personal loan interest rate starting from 10% per annum.

description: The case dates back to the 2019 Essel liquidity crisis, when a sharp fall in Zee Entertainment's share price triggered margin calls on pledged shares securing debt held by six Kotak fixed maturity plans (FMPs). title: “Saving ₹5,000 a month will not change your life”: Why Ranveer Allahbadia’s money advice for people earning under ₹50,000 hits different for women
description: If you earn less than ₹50,000 a month, should your biggest financial goal be finding the perfect mutual fund? Or should you focus on increasing your income first? That question sparked a lively conversation online after YouTuber and podcaster Ranveer Allahbadia shared his thoughts on money, savings and wealth building.In a post on X, Allahbadia argued that people earning under ₹50,000 a month should stop spending so much time worrying about where to invest their money. Instead, he believes their energy is better spent figuring out how to earn more."If you're earning under 50,000 a month, stop obsessing over where to invest. Start obsessing over how to earn more. Saving 5,000 rupees a month will not change your life. Doubling your income will. And never increase your lifestyle as fast as you increase your income. Fix the input before optimizing the output. Something I had to figure out while building BeerBiceps from scratch," he wrote in the post.His post quickly caught people's attention. But amid all this, Ranveer’s advice is resonating with many women across the country.
content:
“Saving ₹5,000 a month will not change your life”: Why Ranveer Allahbadia’s money advice for people earning under ₹50,000 hits different for women
